reasons: Karrie directly punishes Fredrinn's high-HP frontline identity.
Max-HP true damage reduces the value of Fredrinn's durability in extended fights.

proof.summary: Fredrinn wants to survive and convert damage into value, while Karrie stacks Lightwheel Marks to deal max-HP true damage through his tankiness.
worksBestWhen: Karrie has peel and keeps distance from Fredrinn's taunt range.
Karrie attacks Fredrinn before he reaches the backline.
failureCases: Fredrinn catches Karrie with his engage and taunt sequence.
Karrie is forced to fight without frontline protection.

reasons: Baxia weakens Fredrinn's sustain-based fight pattern.
Fredrinn becomes less threatening when his HP recovery is reduced during extended combat.

proof.summary: Fredrinn converts stored damage into HP through damage dealt, while Baxia reduces healing and shield effects to make Fredrinn's sustain less reliable.
worksBestWhen: Baxia stays close enough to apply anti-sustain during the fight.
The team follows with burst or sustained DPS.
failureCases: Baxia cannot keep contact with Fredrinn.
Fredrinn waits out Baxia pressure before committing.

reasons: Dyrroth can reduce Fredrinn's physical defense and punish him before he stabilizes.
This is especially useful in lane skirmishes where Fredrinn has fewer teammates to cover him.

proof.summary: Fredrinn relies on durability to extend fights, while Dyrroth's defense reduction makes him easier to burst during short trades.
worksBestWhen: Dyrroth engages after Fredrinn spends his engage or taunt tools.
failureCases: Dyrroth fights into full Fredrinn combo points.
Fredrinn survives the burst and turns with Appraiser's Wrath.

reasons: X.Borg can pressure Fredrinn without needing to fully commit into his taunt range.
Last Insanity provides true-damage threat against durable targets.

proof.summary: Fredrinn wants enemies to stay in close-range control, while X.Borg can poke, zone, and threaten true damage through Last Insanity.
worksBestWhen: X.Borg keeps distance and uses armor state safely.
X.Borg waits for Fredrinn to spend crowd control before committing ultimate.
failureCases: Fredrinn catches X.Borg after armor is broken.
X.Borg dives too deep and gets taunted before detonation.

reasons: Valir can keep Fredrinn away from the backline and make it harder for him to start taunt combos.
Knockback and slows reduce Fredrinn's ability to force extended melee fights.

proof.summary: Fredrinn wants to enter and hold enemies in close range, while Valir can push him away and slow his approach.
worksBestWhen: Valir holds knockback for Fredrinn's engage.
The team retreats when Valir creates space.
failureCases: Fredrinn flanks or catches Valir first.
Valir's disengage is unavailable during the main engage.
